scoring_points.description: For the correct calculated value: 1 point

required_elements: Provide a correct response from the accepted alternatives

parts.prompt:
Attempting to change the density of the O (g ), the student opens the valve on the side of the cylinder,
pushes down on the piston to release some of the gas, and closes the valve again. The temperature of the gas
remains constant at 25°C. Will this action change the density of the gas remaining in the cylinder? Justify your
answer.
Use a pencil or pen with black or dark blue ink only. Do NOT write your name. Do NOT write outside the box.

scoring_points.description: For the correct answer and a valid justification: 1 point

required_elements: Provide a correct response from the accepted alternatives
alternatives.correct_example: No, the density of the gas remains constant because P, R, and T remain constant AND t he mass and volume of O2 decrease proportionately

parts.prompt:
The student tries to change the density of the O (g ) by cooling the cylinder to −55°C,which causes the
volume of the gas to decrease. Using principles of kinetic molecular theory, explain why the volume of the
O (g ) decreases when the temperature decreases to −55°C.

required_elements: Provide a correct response from the accepted alternatives
alternatives.correct_example: As the gas cools, the average kinetic energy (speed) of the O 2 molecules decreases. The molecules rebound with less energy when they collide with each other and the walls of the container. The spacing between particles decreases, causing the volume occupied by the gas to decrease

parts.prompt:
The student further cools the cylinder to −180°C and observes that the measured volume of the O (g ) is
substantially smaller than the volume that is calculated using the ideal gas law. Assume all equipment is
functioning properly. Explain why the measured volume of the O (g ) is smaller than the calculated volume.
(The boiling point of O (l ) is −183°C.)
Use a pencil or pen with black or dark blue ink only. Do NOT write your name. Do NOT write outside the box.

alternatives.correct_example: The ideal gas law assumes that gas particles do not experience interparticle attractions. As
